programs.name: Medicaid for the Aged, Blind, or Disabled (ABD)
programs.what_it_does: Provides health coverage including long-term care services for eligible seniors and disabled individuals. In Ohio, it covers nursing home care and coordinates with waiver programs for home-based alternatives.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ or disabled individuals with low income and assets meeting Ohio Medicaid eligibility.
programs.benefit_value: Varies; covers medical costs, long-term care up to full eligibility limits (e.g., income ~$2,829/month for 2024 singles)

programs.name: PASSPORT Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services as an alternative to nursing facility placement. Services include personal care, homemaker assistance, adult day care, and medical equipment to support aging in place.
programs.who_its_for: Ohio residents aged 60+ needing nursing home level of care but preferring home-based services, meeting financial eligibility.
programs.benefit_value: Annual cap ~$60,000 per participant; serves ~30,000 Ohio seniors

programs.name: Ohio Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings, including adult day health and home care. Coordinates all Medicare and Medicaid-covered care through interdisciplinary teams.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified for nursing home level care, able to live safely in community, in PACE service areas.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitation coverage; no copays for covered services

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI-1)
programs.what_it_does: Pays Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copays for eligible low-income beneficiaries. QMB covers Part A/B premiums/cost-sharing; SLMB/QI cover Part B premiums.
programs.who_its_for: Medicare beneficiaries with income/assets below Ohio thresholds (e.g., QMB: ~$1,325/month individual 2026).
programs.benefit_value: Part B premium ~$185/month saved; QMB covers up to $8,850/year in cost-sharing

programs.name: Ohio SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly EBT card benefits for purchasing groceries to combat food insecurity. Ohio administers with state-specific deductions and expedited processing.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ (higher income disregard); households with income <130% FPL.

programs.name: Ohio Energy Assistance (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers grants to help pay heating bills and crisis assistance for utility shutoffs. Includes winter walk-through for safety measures.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors; income <175% FPL, priority for 60+ and disabled.
programs.benefit_value: Average grant $400-$600 heating season; crisis up to $1,000

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements like insulation, sealing, and furnace repair. Reduces energy costs and improves health/safety for vulnerable households.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households <200% FPL, priority for seniors 60+, disabled, families with children.
programs.benefit_value: Average savings $300+/year energy bills; services worth $6,000-$8,000/home

programs.name: Ohio Senior Health Insurance Information Program (OSHIIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free counseling on Medicare options, plan comparisons, and claims assistance. Helps seniors save on premiums and out-of-pocket costs through education.
programs.who_its_for: All Ohio Medicare beneficiaries, families, caregivers; special outreach to low-income/rural.

programs.name: Home-Delivered Meals (via Ohio Aging Network)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Often includes safety wellness checks by volunteers.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound adults 60+ unable to prepare meals, often low-income or at nutrition risk.
programs.benefit_value: 5-7 meals/week; federal OAA funds ~200K meals/month statewide

programs.name: National Family Caregiver Support Program (NFCSP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides respite care, counseling, and training to family caregivers. Offers supplemental services like supplies to reduce caregiver burden.
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid family caregivers of adults 60+ or relatives of 55+ with disabilities.
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $500/month; serves 10K+ Ohio caregivers annually

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Subsidizes part-time community service jobs with training for unsubsidized employment. Places participants in nonprofits/public agencies.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income individuals 55+ with income <125% FPL, poor employment prospects.
programs.benefit_value: 20 hours/week at min. wage (~$7,500/year); serves 1K+ Ohio seniors

programs.name: Legal Assistance for Seniors (via Ohio Legal Services)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal help for issues like housing, benefits, abuse, and consumer problems. Operates through Area Agencies on Aging and legal aid networks.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ facing legal barriers to independence.
programs.benefit_value: Thousands of cases/year; recovers $10M+ in benefits

programs.name: Ohio Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities resolving complaints and protecting rights. Provides education and monitoring for quality care.
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, assisted living, and other facilities; families.
